title: Classifier-agent output
description: Structured JSON the classifier-agent emits in the context phase. Consumed by the classifier step, which merges the fields into the run's decisions and short title.

complexity.description: Scope assessment of the actual change (NOT brief length): the signal the engine uses to pick the flow — `simple` routes to a lean flow (single reviewer, no fanout), `complex` to the full panel. Always emit one; this overrides the deterministic length seed.

task_short.description: Short kebab-case slug summarising the task — used for filenames, branch names, commit subjects.

refs_to_load.description: Senior-pattern references picked for this task — basenames from the refs catalog (e.g. 'redis.md'). Delivered to the sandbox at .loom/work/refs/<name> for downstream agents to read.

security_needed.description: Whether the security reviewer agent should run for this task.

antipattern_rules_applicable.description: Anti-pattern rule identifiers from CLAUDE.md that the implementer should respect.

stack.description: LLM-picked stack identification. When present, the classifier's picks override the deterministic stack detector; when null, the table-driven detector is the source of truth.

package_manager.description: Picked from stack-candidates.yaml.package_managers[*].name for the chosen language, or null when no PM applies.

change_kind.description: Classification of the task's diff shape. The review fanout consumes it for reviewer selectivity — an agent that declares relevant_for_change_kinds is dropped when the change_kind is known and not listed (e.g. the challenger skips a config-only or docs-only change).
